sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Fiorentina, sitting at 8th in the league with 59 points, host Empoli, who are struggling in 19th place with just 25 points, in Round 34 of the Serie A at the Stadio Artemio Franchi in Florence. This matchup presents a clear disparity in form and standings, with Fiorentina looking to capitalize on their home advantage. Empoli will be desperate for points to climb out of the relegation zone. The referee for this clash, Antonio Rapuano, averages 5.1 yellow cards per game and 0.36 red cards per game, suggesting a potentially heated encounter.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two sides, draws have dominated with 4 out of 5 games ending level, while Empoli managed a solitary win and Fiorentina have yet to triumph in this period. Their most recent encounter on September 29, 2024, ended in a 0-0 stalemate, with Fiorentina’s Dodô earning Player of the Match honors with a Sofascore rating of 7.8. Historically, these games tend to be low-scoring, as 6 out of the last 7 head-to-heads have seen under 2.5 goals.

sections.subtitle: Fiorentina’s Form and Key Players
sections.text: Fiorentina come into this game with an impressive streak of no losses in their last 8 matches, often scoring first in 6 of their last 8 games and leading at halftime in the same number. They’ve scored 53 goals this season, ranking 6th in the league, though they’ve underperformed their expected goals by -9.24, placing them 18th in over/underperformance. Star forward Moise Kean, despite being absent due to injury, has scored 17 goals (2nd in the league), while Yacine Adli stands out in midfield with 4 goals, 5 assists, and a league rank of 4 for percentage of long balls in total passes at 9.4%. Other notable contributors include Albert Guðmundsson with 6 goals and Robin Gosens with 5 goals and 4 assists, showcasing Fiorentina’s attacking depth. Rolando Mandragora has been in fine form recently, averaging a 7.4 Sofascore rating over the last 5 matches with 2 goals and 2 assists.

sections.subtitle: Empoli’s Struggles and Key Performers
sections.text: Empoli, on the other hand, are in dismal form, winless in their last 9 matches and often conceding first in 6 of their last 8 games. They’ve struggled offensively, though exact goal totals are not provided, and their defense has been leaky with no clean sheets in their last 3 matches. Sebastiano Esposito leads their attack with 8 goals, while Lorenzo Colombo adds 5 goals and 2 assists to the frontline. In midfield, Giuseppe Pezzella has created 10 big chances, ranking 4th in the league, and defender Mattia De Sciglio impresses with a 75% aerial duel win rate, ranking 5th league-wide. Luca Marianucci also stands out with a league rank of 2 for long balls in total passes at 10.7%.

sections.subtitle: Team Statistics and Tactical Insights
sections.text: Fiorentina dominate in several statistical categories, boasting a league-leading 52.45% ground duels won and ranking 3rd in fast breaks per game at 1.14. They’ve created 72 big chances (10th in the league) but missed 44 of them, though their conversion rate of 38.89% ranks 7th. Their goal distribution shows strength in the 46-60 minute period with 28.57% of goals scored then. Empoli, however, struggle defensively, often conceding in the 76-90 minute period with 29.09% of their goals against coming late. Both teams see most matches with over 2.5 goals recently, with Fiorentina at 8 of 10 and Empoli at 4 of 5, hinting at a potentially open game.

sections.subtitle: Injuries and Absences
sections.text: Fiorentina will miss key forward Moise Kean, whose 17 goals and league rank of 2 in scoring make his absence significant, along with midfielders Andrea Colpani and Edoardo Bove. Empoli are also without important players like defender Liberato Cacace and forward Christian Kouamé, which could further weaken their already struggling squad. These absences may tilt the balance further in Fiorentina’s favor, especially in the attacking third.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts and Streaks
sections.text: Fiorentina have a strong record of scoring first and winning the first half in 6 of their last 8 games, while Empoli have consistently been the first to concede in 6 of their last 8. Both teams have a trend of low corner counts, with Fiorentina at under 10.5 corners in 9 of their last 10 matches and Empoli in all 9 of their last 9. Fiorentina also have a history of both teams scoring in 4 of their last 5 home games, which could make for an intriguing battle despite Empoli’s woes.
